note: All cases are `recipe` (response-graded), probing load-bearing permissions, config keys, the toggle route, the access service, events, and the access hooks — grounded in the module source.

evals.prompt: What problem does the Publish Content module solve on a Drupal 11 site, and which entity type does it apply to?
evals.expected_output: It gives granular per-content-type and per-role publish/unpublish permissions so editors can toggle a node's published status without the broad 'administer nodes' permission. It applies to nodes only.

evals.prompt: What is the settings route name and admin path for Publish Content, and which permission is required to reach it?
evals.expected_output: Route publishcontent.settings at /admin/config/workflow/publishcontent, gated by the `access publish content settings` permission.

evals.prompt: I want Publish Content to create a new node revision every time someone toggles publish status. Which config key in publishcontent.settings do I set, and give a drush command.
evals.expected_output: Set create_revision to true: drush config:set publishcontent.settings create_revision 1 -y (config object publishcontent.settings).

evals.prompt: On this site I want authors to unpublish only their own articles. Which Publish Content permission grants that for the 'article' content type?
evals.expected_output: Grant the per-type permission `unpublish own node type article` (checks node owner == current user).

evals.prompt: Name Publish Content's global permission that lets a reviewer publish any content of any type, and the one that only lets them publish content they can already edit.
evals.expected_output: `publish any content` publishes any node of any type; `publish editable content` only publishes nodes the user already has update/edit access to.

evals.prompt: Which route/path toggles a node's published status in Publish Content, and what request protection does it require?
evals.expected_output: Route entity.node.publish at /node/{node}/toggleStatus (translation variant entity.node.publish_translation at /node/{node}/toggleStatus/{langcode}); it requires a valid CSRF token plus the _publish_access_check and hasUiLocalTask access checks.

evals.prompt: In custom code, how do I check whether the current user is allowed to publish/unpublish a given node using Publish Content? Name the service and method.
evals.expected_output: Call the publishcontent.access service: \Drupal::service('publishcontent.access')->access($account, $node) returns an AccessResult; check ->isAllowed().

evals.prompt: Which hooks does Publish Content invite so a module can deny publishing access to a node programmatically?
evals.expected_output: hook_publishcontent_publish_access() and hook_publishcontent_unpublish_access(), each receiving the NodeInterface and AccountInterface and returning an AccessResult (forbidden to block, neutral to abstain).

evals.prompt: I want custom code to react whenever Publish Content publishes or unpublishes a node. Which events are dispatched?
evals.expected_output: The toggle controller dispatches Drupal\publishcontent\Event\PublishContentPublishEvent and PublishContentUnpublishEvent (each exposes public $node and $langcode); subscribe keyed on the class name.

evals.prompt: Publish Content offers two UI ways to toggle status. Which two config keys enable the local task tab and the node-form checkbox, and what are their defaults?
evals.expected_output: ui_localtask (default true) shows the Publish/Unpublish local task tab; ui_checkbox (default false) shows a publish checkbox on the node edit form.

evals.prompt: How can I add a one-click publish/unpublish toggle link to a Views listing of nodes with Publish Content?
evals.expected_output: With Views enabled the module adds a node field 'Publish/Unpublish' (handler id publishcontent_node); add it to a node-based View to render a per-row toggle link that respects publishcontent.access.

evals.prompt: What are Publish Content's module dependencies, and does it require any contrib modules?
evals.expected_output: It depends only on Drupal core's node module; no contrib modules are required.
